isolation: Per-sandbox VM on Railway's VM primitive (specific hypervisor technology not publicly disclosed)
name: Railway Sandboxes
notes: Sandboxes are gated behind Railway's Priority Boarding program; the TypeScript SDK is in beta and may have breaking changes.
price_headline: $50/vCPU-month and $50/GB-memory-month (~$0.0000000193 per vCPU-second or GB-second); egress $0.05/GB
pricing_model: Per-second, billed on vCPU and memory consumed while running
summary: Railway Sandboxes are short-lived Linux virtual machines scoped to a Railway environment that can be provisioned on demand to run commands, read and write files, and then be destroyed. They target agent workloads and untrusted code execution, and are controlled programmatically via the TypeScript SDK, the Railway CLI, or the dashboard. The product reached general availability on June 26, 2026 after a public beta that started May 14, 2026.
